deliverable_target: a faithful, conceptually serious chapter for Financial Markets, plus a per-course compiled PDF book.
visual_target: selected lecture frames are only supporting evidence for slide text, equations, diagrams, chalkboard structure, and institutional examples; do not let them override the transcript.
mathematical_standard: preserve definitions, mechanisms, institutional detail, and any quantitative reasoning from the lecture, but avoid generic filler or invented textbook exposition.
style_target: notes should read like polished companion notes for a serious finance and institutions course, with clear prose, explicit causal structure, and careful distinction between intuition, institutional detail, and analytical claims.
credit_target: keep explicit credit to Robert J. Shiller and curation by LazyingArt LLC; reserve the website URL for the generated front-matter credit, not the prose body.
front_matter_single: Original lecture by Robert J. Shiller. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
front_matter_plural: Original lectures by Robert J. Shiller. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
reference_missing_text: No directly matching reference PDF is available for this course.
reference_no_match_text: Matching course reference PDFs were found, but no strong topical excerpt was automatically matched for this lecture.
reference_budget_text: Matching course reference PDFs were found, but no excerpt fit within the current prompt budget.
